mnemonics: Cantor-Schröder-Bernstein (CSB) says: If you can 'inject' into each other, your 'sizes' are equal. Remember: 'Injection for Equivalence'.
Use the 'Hotel Infinity' thought experiment: If you can move all guests from one infinite hotel to another without anyone being left out (a bijection), the hotels are the same 'cardinal size'.
keyPoints: The order relation |A| ≤ |B| is defined by the existence of an injective function from A to B.
The Cantor-Schröder-Bernstein Theorem states that if |A| ≤ |B| and |B| ≤ |A|, then |A| = |B| (i.e., there exists a bijection).
For any set A, its power set P(A) has a strictly greater cardinality, |A| < |P(A)|, which establishes a hierarchy of infinities.
commonMistakes: Assuming all infinite sets have the same cardinality (e.g., thinking |ℝ| = |ℚ|).
Confusing the order relation for cardinal numbers (based on injections) with the order for natural numbers.
